Verbalization of LLM -- Results

Built 2026-05-15 16:39 from current eval JSONs in creative/analysis/ and ao/analysis/.

Overview

Project: Mismatch in Human–Machine Communication: A Case Study on Implicit Constraints in Instruction Following (NeurIPS 2026 in progress).

When an LLM responds to a free-form prompt, it commits to many properties of its output the prompt never specifies — a character's name, a story's POV, a code snippet's side effects. We compare three bridges for recovering these implicit commitments:

  • Self-report (SR): ask the model the question directly. With and without question-form SFT.
  • Activation Oracle (AO): Karvonen-style activation injection from the prompt's residual stream into a question-answering forward pass. With and without further LoRA training.
  • NLA (Natural Language Autoencoder): released Anthropic / kitft AV that maps an activation vector → free-form natural-language description. Inference only — different base model (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, layer 20).

We run the same pipeline (same 100 prompts, same 75/25 split, same canonical 11Q + discovered ~14Q) on three model families: Qwen3-8B, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, and Gemma-2-9B-IT. See the Cross-model tab for current numbers across all three.

This page is auto-generated from the eval JSONs each time you run python results/build_html.py.

Setup & settings

Train / test split

One source of truth: creative/analysis/prompt_split_75_25.json (seed=0). 100 prompts total, split per prompt (not per question), so all questions associated with a given prompt go to the same side. Question identity is not held out — only prompts.

Train prompts (75):
0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 16, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 28, 29, 30, 31, 34, 35, 37, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 46, 47, 48, 50, 52, 54, 55, 56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 63, 66, 67, 69, 70, 71, 72, 73, 75, 76, 78, 80, 81, 83, 84, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 98, 99

Test prompts (25):
5, 12, 17, 18, 27, 32, 33, 36, 38, 39, 45, 49, 51, 53, 61, 62, 64, 65, 68, 74, 77, 79, 82, 85, 97

  • Train: ~1871 items across the 75 prompts (each prompt has ~25 questions: 11 canonical + ~14 LLM-discovered)
  • Test: ~619 items across the 25 prompts

Bridge settings

BridgeStatusWhat it is
Untrained SRavailableBare Qwen3-8B answering the question directly with the chat template — no LoRA, no activation injection. Lower-bound for the verbal channel.
Trained SRavailableQwen3-8B with a LoRA fine-tuned on the 75 train prompts of the merged canonical+discovered QA pool (rank 16, alpha 32, lr 5e-5, 5 epochs).
AO baselineavailableBare Qwen3-8B + the layer-18→layer-1 activation-injection mechanism with NO LoRA at all. Tests how much signal the injection mechanism alone carries.
Frozen AOavailableQwen3-8B with the Karvonen LatentQA oracle LoRA loaded, no further training. The general-purpose oracle from prior work.
Trained AOavailableQwen3-8B starting from the Karvonen oracle, further LoRA-trained on the 75 train prompts of the merged pool (rank 64, alpha 128, lr 1e-5, 20 epochs; best by overall test accuracy at epoch 6).
Trained AO (scratch)availableQwen3-8B with a fresh LoRA (no Karvonen bootstrap) trained on the same 75 train prompts. Tests how much the Karvonen oracle is doing for us.
MajorityavailableFor each answer type, predict the modal value computed on the TRAIN split, scored against test items with the same per-type grader the real models use. See the 'Majority Baseline' tab.

Question types present

Answer typen (total)

Majority baseline

For each answer type we compute a per-type modal value from the train split and apply that constant prediction to the test set, scoring with the same per-type grader the real models are scored with.

  • binary, pov, tense, title_format, multiple_choice, name: modal value of the train-set gold strings (normalized lowercase). Test-side exact-match grader.
  • length: predict median(train p50s). Score: test_p10 ≤ predicted ≤ test_p90.
  • name_list: predict the modal single name across all train name-lists.
  • title_text: predict the modal title across all train titles_all.
  • short_text: exact match against per-item unique short text, so majority is near-zero by design.
Typen trainn testModal valueTest accuracy

length300100median(train p50) = 191.50.000
multiple_choice22678modal train value = 'yes'0.026
name7525modal train value = 'clara'0.160

pov7525modal train value = '3p'1.000
short_text7530modal train value = 'a remote coastal cliff or island'0.000
tense7525modal train value = 'past'1.000
title_format7525modal train value = 'bold_md'0.800
title_text7525modal train title = 'The Edge of the World'0.080

NLA

NLA explanations browser

Per-token natural-language descriptions of the residual stream at every user-content token of each chat-formatted prompt. Extractor: Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, layer 20 (= hidden_states[21]), add_generation_prompt=False (matches AO source extraction). AV: kitft/nla-qwen2.5-7b-L20-av, greedy decode, max_new_tokens=200.

⚠ NLA uses a different base model (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) and a different layer than the AO/SR side (Qwen3-8B at L18). The prompts are the same, the activations are not. NLA descriptions are exploratory, not directly comparable to AO/SR accuracies.

3663 per-position records across 100 prompts. Sections: system=2100, user_content=1063, user_end=200, user_role=300. Parsed <explanation>: 3363/3663 overall, 1063/1063 on user-content positions.

Source file on disk: /net/projects2/chai-lab/smallyan/imp_data/nla_qwen25_all_positions/all_positions_explanations.jsonl (includes all sections: system, user_role, user_content, user_end).

Tokens with the no-expl faded style had a truncated or malformed AV output (no closed <explanation> tag). Click them anyway to see the message.

NLA judge — does the explanation contain the answer?

We send each NLA explanation to gpt-5.4-mini with a strict grader prompt: "does the explanation EXPLICITLY state the gold answer? Don't infer or guess." Three labels: correct, wrong, not_included. Scope: 25 test prompts × ~25 questions (canonical 11 + LLM-discovered).

Setting A = judge the explanation at the last chat-formatted token only. Setting B = judge every user-content token's explanation independently, then collapse to a per-(prompt, qid) sequence label using the rule: both correct & wrong tokens → conflict; only correct → correct; only wrong → wrong; otherwise not_included.

Setting A: 629 items · correct=0, wrong=7, not_included=622
Setting B per-token: 6448 items · correct=14, wrong=34, not_included=6400
Setting B per-sequence: 629 sequences · correct=7, wrong=23, conflict=0, not_included=599

NLA constraint extractor (open-vocab, strict no-overread)

Per-token: each user-content token's NLA explanation is sent to gpt-5.4-mini with a definition of "implicit constraint" + 5 few-shot examples (synthetic, no test/train prompts leaked) + an anti-over-reading rule. The LLM emits a flat list of complete-sentence constraint statements, or an empty list when the description only contains prompt-paraphrase / prompt topic / token-level next-word commentary.

Source: /net/projects2/chai-lab/smallyan/imp_data/nla_qwen25_all_positions/all_positions_explanations.jsonl. Outputs: nla/data/constraints_per_token{,_train}.jsonl.

splitpromptstokensnon-emptytotal constraintslast-token non-empty
Test (25 prompts)2533141 (12.4%)1390/25
Train (75 prompts)751032129 (12.5%)4466/75

Verification: appearance rate across the 100 generated stories per prompt

For each unique extracted constraint sentence, gpt-5.4-mini judges all 100 sampled stories for that prompt and reports the fraction that satisfy the constraint. Buckets: all rate=1.00 (perfect commitment), high 0.85-0.99 (strong commitment), medium 0.50-0.85 (partial), low 0.15-0.50 (weakly predictive), noise <0.15 (likely NLA-extractor hallucination). The high/medium/low/all thresholds match creative/label_consistency.py; the noise split isolates clear over-reads.

splitverifiedmean rateall (=1.00)high (0.85-0.99)medium (0.50-0.85)low (0.15-0.50)noise (<0.15)
Test1390.6940 (28.8%)37 (26.6%)21 (15.1%)16 (11.5%)25 (18.0%)
Trainverification not run yet

Per-prompt browser

Pick a prompt below. Tokens whose explanation surfaced an extractable constraint are highlighted; empty ones are faded. Click any token to see its explanation and the extracted constraints. Each extracted constraint now also shows its appearance rate — the fraction of the 100 sampled stories for that prompt that satisfy the constraint (gpt-5.4-mini grades each story individually).

Code locations

  • creative/build_merged_qa.py — merges canonical + discovered into the 2490-item benchmark + 75/25 split
  • ao/new_introspection_training/{train,eval}.py — merged-pool AO trainer and evaluator
  • new_introspect_training/{train,build_sft_data,eval}.py — merged-pool self-report SFT pipeline
  • nla/run_all_prompts.py — NLA last-token L20 inference pipeline
  • nla_inference/ — local fork of kitft/natural_language_autoencoders
  • results/build_html.py — this page's builder
